title: Pulse UI bug batch — logo underline, drop mediaUrl, body→summary, swipe tabs, archived vote status, Clerk avatar
description:
Six discrete bugs reported in one session:
1. Header logo Link is rendered with the default anchor underline from globals.css; should be flush.
2. Drop mediaUrl from the feed_items schema, ingest endpoint, list DTO, components, and tests.
3. Add length limits on title/summary and rename body→summary across schema, API, DTO, and components.
4. Swiping left/right on the feed surface should switch between FEED and ARCHIVED tabs.
5. Archived items always show LIKED/DIDN'T (vote status); load vote with archived list and derive status from it instead of defaulting to READ.
6. UserSheet sidebar avatar should display user.imageUrl from Clerk; fall back to initial when no image.

title: Make site header scroll-aware blur
description: Design intent: header is solid paper when at top of scroll; only switches to translucent 82% + blur(14px) saturate(140%) once scrolled. Current header blurs unconditionally which gives a softer/glassier look than design.html specifies. Fix: track window scroll, toggle between solid var(--paper) and color-mix translucent.
close_reason: header now matches design: solid paper when at scroll top, blur+translucent only after scroll; padding switched from h-12 to py-3 to match design's padding: 12px 16px

title: chore: production deployment docs and env audit
description: Document deployment on Vercel with managed Postgres. List every env var (DATABASE_URL, CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, CLERK_SECRET_KEY, ALLOWED_OWNER_USER_ID, INGEST_TOKEN) with purpose and rotation guidance. Add a build-time env audit step that fails when any required var is missing. Provide a curl example for ingest including auth and rate-limit headers.
acceptance_criteria: DEPLOYMENT.md exists and covers Vercel project setup, managed Postgres provisioning, env var list, ingest token rotation, and database migration on deploy. `bun run check:env` runs as part of the production build script and fails missing-var deploys. Curl example for ingest is documented.
notes: Refs R17, R18, AC 'production deployment requirements documented'.

title: chore: mobile/touch UI acceptance gate via Playwright
description: Codify the scope's 'mobile/touch UI checks' acceptance gate as a runnable Playwright suite at 360px viewport: card readability, touch target sizing (>= 44px for actionable elements), no hover-only interactions in the unread feed, history view, and feedback interactions. Run against a built dev server with seeded data.
acceptance_criteria: Playwright runs in CI-shaped mode. Touch-target audit fails build when any actionable element drops below 44px. Hover-only assertion fails build when a feed action requires hover. Suite covers unread, history, and feedback flows.
notes: Refs AC 'mobile/touch UI checks cover card readability, touch target sizing, and core feed interactions'.

title: feat: History view for read items with cursor pagination
description: Implement the route consuming GET /api/feed/history. Same FeedCard component; visually distinguishes read state without relying only on color. Cursor pagination. Empty state. Navigation entry from the main feed shell.
acceptance_criteria: Renders only read items in deterministic recency order. Cursor pagination works. Visual distinction passes the 'color is not the only signal' a11y check. Tests cover empty and paginated cases.
notes: Refs R5, R13, design journey 'Review Read Items'.

title: feat: thumbs feedback UI with optional reasoning sheet
description: Wire the FeedCard thumbs controls to PUT /api/feed/:id/feedback. Tapping a thumb submits optimistically; tapping the same thumb again clears it; tapping the opposite thumb switches it. Long-press or secondary affordance opens a reasoning sheet (shadcn Sheet) for optional text. Failures roll back the optimistic state.
acceptance_criteria: Submit, switch, and clear flows each work and persist. Reasoning sheet appears with a touch-friendly affordance. Optimistic updates roll back on server failure. Tests cover each interaction.
notes: Refs R6, R15, R22, design journey 'Give Taste Feedback'.

title: feat: FeedCard component with all required states and a11y
description: Implement the reusable FeedCard React component used by both unread and history views. Renders title, source name/URL, optional media (graceful fallback when URL fails to load), TLDR body, relative time, read/unread affordance, open-source action, thumbs up/down controls, and a reasoning-input affordance. Implements all states from the design artifact: default unread, default read, with-media, without-media, skeleton loading, error overlay. Touch targets >= 44px. Color is never the only state signal. Accessible labels on thumbs controls.
acceptance_criteria: Each state has a unit test asserting the expected DOM. axe-core a11y check passes for every state. Touch-target audit passes (>= 44px for interactive elements). Color contrast meets WCAG AA. Stories or test fixtures cover every state for visual review.
notes: Refs R7, R14, R15, design 'Card Anatomy', a11y requirements.

title: feat: Unread feed view with cursor scroll and optimistic mark-read
description: Implement the main route consuming GET /api/feed/unread. Cursor-driven infinite scroll loads more pages at the boundary. Mark-read is optimistic and rolls back on failure. Empty state ('Nothing new to inspect'), error state, skeleton loading. Pull-to-refresh on touch devices.
acceptance_criteria: Renders unread items sorted newest first. Scroll to bottom loads the next page using the cursor. Tapping mark-read updates UI immediately and reverts on server failure. Empty/error/loading states render correctly. Tests cover the optimistic-update rollback path.
notes: Refs R3, R12, R13, design states + journeys 'Inspect Today's Signals'.

title: feat: Tailwind v4 + shadcn install, editorial design tokens, Clerk-gated app shell
description: Install Tailwind v4 (via the project's existing PostCSS config) and the shadcn primitives needed for v1 (Card, Button, Sheet, Skeleton, Toast). Define editorial design tokens (palette, typography, spacing) inspired by effect.institute and kitlangton.com — high-contrast, sparse, writing-forward. Build the App Router root layout with Clerk owner gate, mobile-first viewport meta, and a base header containing only what's needed for the single-feed UI.
acceptance_criteria: Tailwind v4 utilities work in a sample page. shadcn primitives render. Design tokens are exposed via CSS variables. Unauthenticated users hit the Clerk sign-in flow. Authenticated owner sees the empty app shell. Tests cover authed/unauthed shell rendering.
notes: Refs R11, R16, design constraints (mobile-first, touch-first, single feed).

title: feat: PUT /api/feed/:id/feedback mutable thumbs and reasoning
description: Implement Effect-handled PUT /api/feed/:id/feedback. Upserts a single feedback row per item. Body { vote: 'up'|'down'|null, reasoning: string|null }. Null vote clears the feedback. Returns the updated feedback record.
acceptance_criteria: First submission creates feedback. Subsequent submission updates it (latest wins). vote=null clears the record. 404 when item id not found. Validation rejects invalid vote values. Contract tests cover create/update/clear/404/validation.
notes: Refs R6, R15, R22, AC 'submit thumbs feedback', 'existing feedback can be updated and cleared'.

title: feat: PATCH /api/feed/:id/read to toggle read state
description: Implement Effect-handled PATCH /api/feed/:id/read. Body { read: boolean }. Sets read_at to now() when read=true; sets to NULL when read=false. Returns the updated item. 404 if item not found.
acceptance_criteria: Toggling read=true sets read_at. read=false clears it. Idempotent. 404 when id not found. Contract tests cover all paths.
notes: Refs R4, AC 'marking item read removes from main feed'.

title: feat: GET /api/feed/unread with cursor pagination
description: Implement Effect-handled GET /api/feed/unread. Returns the page of unread items (read_at IS NULL) sorted by (created_at DESC, id DESC). Cursor: base64(created_at|id) tuple. Default page size 20 (constant); accept ?limit up to 50. Response: { items, nextCursor }.
acceptance_criteria: Empty DB returns empty items and null cursor. Items beyond the page size are excluded from the page and a nextCursor is returned. Passing the returned cursor returns the next page deterministically. Read items are excluded. Contract tests cover all four cases.
notes: Refs R3, R12, R21, AC 'main feed shows unread sorted newest', 'list returns bounded page and cursor'.

title: feat: GET /api/feed/history with cursor pagination
description: Implement Effect-handled GET /api/feed/history. Returns read items (read_at IS NOT NULL) sorted by (read_at DESC, id DESC). Cursor and page-size shape identical to /unread.
acceptance_criteria: Returns only read items in deterministic recency order. Cursor pagination works as in /unread. Contract tests cover the read-only filter and pagination.
notes: Refs R5, R13, AC 'read/history accessible outside main feed'.

title: feat: POST /api/ingest endpoint with URL deduplication
description: Implement the Effect-handled POST /api/ingest endpoint. Validate request body (title, source_url, source_name, optional media_url, body) with Effect Schema. Persist via Drizzle. On unique-constraint violation, return 409 with a clear conflict response referencing the existing item. Idempotent on source_url.
acceptance_criteria: Valid request creates a feed item and returns 201 with the new id. Validation failure returns 400. Missing/invalid token returns 401. Duplicate source_url returns 409 with the existing item id; no new row is created. Contract tests cover all four paths.
notes: Refs R2, R7, R19, AC 'valid token ingest creates', 'invalid token does not create', 'dup URL returns conflict'.

title: feat: Clerk owner authentication and single-owner gate
description: Install @clerk/nextjs. Configure middleware to gate every owner route (everything outside the agent ingest path). Add an ALLOWED_OWNER_USER_ID env var and enforce that only that Clerk user can access owner routes — other authenticated users get a 403. Add server-side helpers for fetching the owner user in Effect handlers.
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated request to an owner route is redirected to sign-in. Authenticated non-owner request gets 403. Authenticated owner request proceeds. Tests cover all three paths.
notes:
Refs R10, R18, AC 'authenticated owner can access'.
Stacked PR opened: https://github.com/theseifhassan/pulse/pull/5 (base feat/effect/pulse-my8.3-effect-skeleton). Validation: vitest 46/46, typecheck clean, biome clean.

title: feat: agent ingest token verification and per-token rate limit middleware
description: Add a bearer-token authentication layer for the /api/ingest path that is separate from Clerk owner auth. Read INGEST_TOKEN from env; reject requests without a valid bearer with 401. Add a basic per-token rate limit (e.g., 60 req/min in-memory; documented as fine for v1 single-instance). Document rotation procedure (set new INGEST_TOKEN, redeploy).
acceptance_criteria: Request without bearer is rejected 401. Request with wrong bearer is rejected 401. Valid bearer passes. Requests beyond the configured rate are rejected 429. Rotation procedure is documented in README and AGENTS.md.
notes: Refs R2, R18, R24, scope Decision Tree (token rotation, rate limiting).

title: feat: Postgres + Drizzle schema and migrations for feed_items and feedback
description: Install drizzle-orm, drizzle-kit, and a Postgres driver. Define the v1 schema: feed_items (id, title, source_url UNIQUE, source_name, media_url nullable, body, created_at, read_at nullable) and feedback (feed_item_id PK, vote enum 'up'|'down' nullable, reasoning text nullable, updated_at). Write the initial migration. Add a migration round-trip test that runs against a real Postgres (testcontainers or a documented local pg).
acceptance_criteria: Drizzle config builds. `bun run db:generate` produces the initial migration. `bun run db:migrate` applies migrations to a local Postgres. Unique constraint on source_url is enforced (insert of duplicate URL raises). Round-trip test passes in CI-shaped run.
notes: Refs R8, R9, R19, R20, R22, R23. The unique source_url constraint is the dedup primitive.

title: feat: Effect v4 beta service skeleton with Postgres layer and error model
description: Install Effect v4 beta at a PINNED exact version (no caret/tilde). Define the service skeleton used by every API handler: a PostgresClient layer wrapping Drizzle, a typed error union (e.g., NotFound, Conflict, Unauthorized, RateLimited, ValidationError), and a request-handler glue for Next.js Route Handlers that maps Effect failures to HTTP responses. Provide a layered runtime that endpoints will compose.
acceptance_criteria: Effect v4 beta is pinned (no range prefix in package.json or bun.lock). A trivial Effect program using the PostgresClient layer runs in a test. Error model unit tests cover the mapping from each typed error to an HTTP status.
notes: Refs R1, R25. Foundation for all API items.

title: chore: bootstrap test runner and validated env
description: Establish the project's testing and configuration foundation that every other v1 item depends on. Add vitest as the unit/contract test runner, install Playwright for mobile/touch acceptance gates (browsers not yet downloaded). Add a Zod (or Effect Schema) env-var validation module that fails fast in dev and in build when required vars are missing. Add a 'test' script to package.json (the scope flagged it as missing) plus a 'check:env' script. Document the env schema in README.md.
acceptance_criteria: `bun run test` executes the vitest test suite. `bun run test:e2e` runs Playwright in headed/headless modes (with Playwright browsers installable via a documented command). `bun run check:env` exits non-zero when a required var is unset. README documents every required env var.
notes: Refs scope R18 (env vars), constraint 'no test script configured'. No app behavior yet — pure scaffolding.

title: refine: address Pulse v1 scope PR review feedback
description: Apply fixes from review of PR #1 (Pulse v1 scope): rewrite README, drop stale git-remote blocker, add Effect v4 beta risk note, pre-resolve scope gaps for media storage, pagination, feedback mutability, URL dedup, ingest token rotation, rate limiting, and data retention. Tighten design loading-state guidance.
close_reason: Applied PR #1 scope review fixes: rewrote README for Pulse with bun, removed stale git-remote blocker lines, added Effect v4 beta risk acknowledgment, pre-resolved scope gaps (media-by-URL, cursor pagination, URL dedup, feedback mutability, retention, ingest token rotation, rate limiting), tightened design loading-state and media constraints. Validation passed: bunx tsc --noEmit, bunx biome format ., bun run lint.

title: chore: switch Anvil review binding to local review
description: Update the project-owned Anvil workflow configuration to remove Greptile from review bindings and rely on local review instead.
close_reason: Anvil review binding now uses local review with no external Greptile backend.

title: feat: define Pulse curated signal feed
description:
Source summary:
You are helping me build Pulse.

Pulse is my private “Twitter-like” signal feed where Layla, my AI assistant, is the only poster. The goal is not a social network or generic read-it-later app. It is a high-signal curated feed of things I should inspect: technical discoveries, library releases, articles, tweets/threads, videos, ad business/news, Skydeo-relevant market intelligence, tools, workflows, and strategic observations.

Core product idea:
- Layla finds something worth my attention.
- Layla creates a compact recommendation card.
- I read/inspect it.
- I give lightweight feedback.
- Feedback improves Layla’s future curation taste.

Pulse should answer:
“What should Seif know or inspect today, and why?” 

The project UI should be inspired from https://www.effect.institute, https://www.effect.solutions, and https://www.kitlangton.com. It should be mobile first and optimized for touch based devices.

I should be able to manually add feed items too.

Channel:
chat

Captured at:
2026-05-18

Captured by:
user

Context:
User is building Pulse. References provided by user: https://www.effect.institute, https://www.effect.solutions, https://www.kitlangton.com.

Acceptance:
Not defined during intake.

References:
https://www.effect.institute
https://www.effect.solutions
https://www.kitlangton.com
notes:
Scope PR opened for Phase A:
- https://github.com/theseifhassan/pulse/pull/1
- Branch: chore/scope/pulse-my8-scope-pulse-v1
- Commit: chore(scope): scope Pulse v1 [pulse-my8]

Refs pulse-my8
close_reason: v1 complete. All 17 child items merged to master. 108/108 tests passing. Build clean.

title: Configure Anvil workflow files
description: Set up Anvil bindings, templates, shared rules, and readiness cache for this repository using beads, graphite, greptile, local markdown artifacts, and scripted validation.
close_reason: Anvil workflow files were written under .claude; readiness cache records blocked external checks for missing git remote, Greptile registration, and test script.
